romania.title: 😂 George Simion a spart geamurile la Digi24, să-l mai dea și pe el la televizor
romania.summary: Nemulțumit că nu mai apare suficient pe post, George Simion a decis să rezolve problema direct, spărgând mai multe geamuri ale sediului Digi24 pentru a-și asigura câteva minute de transmisie în direct. Momentul a fost exploatat imediat de Prelipceanu, care a spus că oamenii politici ar face orice ca să intre la el în studio, […] The post George Simion a spart geamurile la Digi24, să-l mai dea și pe el la televizor appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: Sectorul financiar-bancar respinge acuzațiile de manipulare a ROBOR: „Concluziile Consiliului Concurenței reprezintă un abuz”
romania.summary: Asociația Română a Băncilor și Federația Patronală a Serviciilor Financiare din România resping, într-o scrisoare deschisă, acuzațiile privind presupusa manipulare a ROBOR și susțin că decizia Consiliului Concurenței de a sancționa participanții la piață riscă să afecteze încrederea publică, stabilitatea financiară și credibilitatea investițională a României.

romania.title: 😂 O clinică de beauty a deversat botox în canalizare și Bucureștiul s-a umplut de șobolani botoxați
romania.summary: Situație neverosimilă în București în zona unei clinici de estetică și tratamente cu botox. Din cauza unei deversări necontrolate de botox în canalizare, zona s-a umplut de șobolani botoxați, cu buze lucioase, care provoacă erecții la prima privire, pentru că insinuează un blow job super alunecos, acesta fiind principalul scop al botoxului. Mulți bucureșteni încearcă […] The post O clinică de beauty a deversat botox în canalizare și Bucureștiul s-a umplut de șobolani botoxați appeared first on Times New Roman .

Ca să terminăm acest bâlci politic ridicol, Nicușor Dan i-a cerut lui Veștea să-și depună mandatul de premier desemnat și i-a încredințat formarea Guvernului unui om serios, dintr-o bucată, cu un CV impresionant: Klaus Werner Iohannis! „Klaus e versat într-ale politicii, a mai avut funcții la vârf în statul […] The post Ca să terminăm cu bâlciul politic, Nicușor l-a propus premier pe Iohannis appeared first on Times New Roman .

medical.title: For babies hospitalized with bronchiolitis, lying on stomach shows no clear benefit over lying on back
medical.summary: Placing a baby on their stomach in the prone position can help them breathe more comfortably. With the chest facing down and the back facing up, this position takes pressure off the lungs, allowing for better oxygen exchange and helping fluid drain more effectively. In a randomized clinical trial, named PROPOSITIS, researchers investigated whether the prone sleeping position could help very young babies with a lung infection called bronchiolitis, not to be confused with bronchitis.

medical.title: Engineered probiotic heads to human trials
medical.summary: A probiotic bioengineered by UBC Okanagan researchers to thrive in the inflamed gut is set to enter human clinical trials in Australia this summer, capping a decade-long effort to turn a beneficial bacterium into a treatment for inflammatory bowel disease.

medical.title: Think you're eating healthy? You may be missing this heart-protecting nutrient
medical.summary: Eating five servings of fruits and vegetables may not be enough if you're missing foods rich in flavanols, a group of compounds linked to better heart health. Researchers found that choices like blackberries, plums, apples, broad beans, cherries, and green tea can dramatically increase flavanol intake.

medical.title: Examining the cardioprotective effects of heart failure treatments in patients with cancer
medical.summary: Patients receiving cancer treatment often face the added complication of side effects affecting the heart, which can lead to the need to discontinue anticancer therapy, reducing its effectiveness. Researchers from the Erasmus University Medical Center, Rotterdam, the Netherlands, presented their findings on the protective effects of medical therapies recommended to treat heart failure at ESC Cardio-Oncology 2026.

medical.title: Why don't some people get vaccinated? It's more complicated than you think
medical.summary: When vaccination rates drop—as is the case with adult influenza vaccinations in Canada and child measles vaccinations in British Columbia—the explanation is often that people are "hesitant."

medical.title: Pilot study suggests cash support may help heart patients stay on medications
medical.summary: A pilot study led by UTSW researchers suggests modest financial support may help low-income heart failure (HF) patients stay on their medications after leaving the hospital. The study, published in the Journal of the American College of Cardiology, enrolled 153 patients at Parkland Memorial Hospital within two weeks of HF hospitalization and randomly assigned them to receive either usual care or a one-time $500 payment.

medical.title: Scientists reprogram brain immune cells to fight Alzheimer’s
medical.summary: A newly identified molecule called OLE helped restore the brain’s immune cells to a more protective state in Alzheimer’s models. The treatment reduced toxic plaque buildup and improved memory, raising hopes for a new therapeutic approach.

medical.title: New tool maps how T cells move within tumors
medical.summary: Researchers at UT Southwestern Medical Center led a study to develop a computational method that reveals how immune cells navigate the complex environment inside tumors, offering insights that could inform future cancer therapies. T cells are key players in the body's defense against cancer, but many tumors create barriers that limit their ability to enter and function effectively.

science.title: Messi or Ronaldo? Your political ideology may play a part
science.summary: Whether someone prefers Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo—arguably two of the world's greatest footballers today—may be associated with their political outlook, according to the results of an international survey led by Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore).

science.title: Bullet Cluster observations reopen dark matter debate with MOND-compatible explanation
science.summary: The Bullet Cluster has so far been considered evidence of the existence of dark matter. An international team of researchers has now analyzed new data and current images from the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST). According to the team, the observations are also consistent with an alternative explanation that does not involve dark matter. If the latter is, in fact, present, it is likely to be in smaller quantities than postulated so far.

science.title: How animals communicate to work together across species boundaries
science.summary: An international team of researchers have published a new review in Animal Behavior revealing how communication enables cooperation between different animal species. The review, titled "The ecology and evolution of cues and signals in animal interspecies cooperation," highlights how movements, visual displays, calls, and other behavioral cues and signals help partners coordinate interactions and align interests across species boundaries.

science.title: Unearthed bathhouse reveals a thriving Roman Nijmegen: 'The Romans did not regard this city as a backwater'
science.summary: Excavations in Nijmegen-West have uncovered large sections of a Roman bathhouse. It is the largest bathhouse complex from the Roman period in the Netherlands. Radboud researcher Stephan Mols can often be found at the excavation site. "The new finds show that the Romans did not regard this city as a backwater. The buildings were even larger and more imposing than we had previously thought."

science.title: Scientists expected a black hole but found a neutrino factory powered by stars
science.summary: A distant galaxy nicknamed Shadow Blaster may have revealed a surprising source of cosmic neutrinos: extreme star formation instead of a supermassive black hole. The discovery suggests that hidden, dust-filled starburst galaxies could account for a significant fraction of the Universe’s high-energy neutrinos.

science.title: Student makes first recorded sighting of a microwhip scorpion in the Daintree Rainforest
science.summary: A James Cook University Ph.D. student's late-night solo survey has led to the first recorded sighting of a microwhip scorpion in the Daintree Rainforest. JCU entomologist and taxonomist Matthew Connors works at the university's Daintree Rainforest Observatory as a demonstrator and rainforest tour guide for visiting groups.

science.title: Small forest fragments can protect more birds when the surrounding landscape is more helpful
science.summary: Larger areas contain more species. This is one of the most ironclad laws of ecology, which explains why large natural areas usually receive higher priority in conservation strategies. In fragmented landscapes, this logic has also led small forest fragments to be seen as environments of lower value for biodiversity.

science.title: Researchers found a Wordle strategy that wins 99% of the time
science.summary: Researchers developed a Wordle-solving strategy that succeeds 99% of the time by focusing on information gain rather than likely answers. The method uses Shannon entropy to identify guesses that reveal the most about the hidden word. Each guess is designed to slash uncertainty and narrow the possibilities faster. The result significantly outperformed more traditional Wordle tactics.

science.title: Men make up less than 18% of Australian primary school teachers. Is this a problem?
science.summary: This week, a group of Australian primary school principals called for more male primary school teachers, saying boys needed more "male role models."

science.title: Microbes destroyed an ancient pterosaur's wingbone, then preserved it for 100 million years
science.summary: More than 100 million years ago, a flying reptile called a pterosaur flew over the oceans hunting squid and fish.

science.title: New research finds major gaps between EU anti-deforestation rules and realities on the ground in Brazil
science.summary: The U.K. could face significant challenges if it follows the European Union's lead and introduces tougher restrictions on imports linked to deforestation, according to new research from The University of Manchester. The study examines how the EU's landmark Regulation on Deforestation-Free Products (EUDR) is likely to work in practice in Brazil's beef sector, which is one of the world's largest sources of beef exports and a major driver of tropical deforestation.

science.title: Einstein’s “biggest blunder” may finally have an explanation
science.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising connection between quantum gravity and an exotic quantum state of matter that could explain why the universe isn’t expanding wildly fast. The study suggests that the very shape of space-time may protect the cosmological constant from disruptive quantum effects.

science.title: DNA time stamps reveal the strawberry’s surprising origins
science.summary: Researchers have created a new way to reconstruct the evolutionary history of complex plant genomes by analyzing genetic traces left by transposable elements. The technique revealed that modern strawberries were assembled through multiple ancient genome-merging events, shedding new light on how major crop species evolved.

environment.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
environment.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

environment.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
environment.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

environment.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
environment.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

environment.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
environment.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

environment.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
environment.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

environment.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
environment.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

environment.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
environment.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

environment.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
environment.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

Evenimentul organizat de Fundația Comunitară Galați a adunat ambasadori a zeci de cauze pentru care alergătorii participanți au făcut tot posibilul să ajungă la finish. Pe lângă emoțiile și încurajările care au condimentat cursele din cadrul maratonului, pentru Fundația Motivation evenimentul a arătat așa: 14 utilizatori de scaun rulant... The post Pentru Fundația Motivation, duminica trecută a fost despre fapte bune și, mai ales, despre o echipă unită în jurul unui scop dedicat binelui. appeared first on Fundatia Motivation Romania .

mix_items.summary: Nemulțumit că nu mai apare suficient pe post, George Simion a decis să rezolve problema direct, spărgând mai multe geamuri ale sediului Digi24 pentru a-și asigura câteva minute de transmisie în direct. Momentul a fost exploatat imediat de Prelipceanu, care a spus că oamenii politici ar face orice ca să intre la el în studio, […] The post George Simion a spart geamurile la Digi24, să-l mai dea și pe el la televizor appeared first on Times New Roman .

mix_items.summary: Zbor Hub Brașov a găzduit finala națională Arena Urșilor Junior 2026, o competiție dedicată liceenilor cu idei de afaceri curajoase și planuri antreprenoriale inovatoare. Brașovul a devenit, pentru câteva ore, … Read More The post Brașovul a devenit capitala tinerilor antreprenori din România la Arena Urșilor Junior 2026 appeared first on Stiri pozitive .

mix_items.summary: București, 20 mai 2026: Fundația World Vision România, împreună cu Liceul Agricol „Sandu Aldea” din Călărași, a inaugurat o seră... mix_items.summary: Atelierele în comunitate organizate de Fundația Inocenți îi ajută pe copii să își dezvolte încrederea în sine, abilitățile emoționale și relațiile sociale. The post Ateliere în comunitate, sprijin pentru dezvoltarea copiilor appeared first on Fundația Inocenți .

mix_items.title: Messi or Ronaldo? Your political ideology may play a part
mix_items.summary: Whether someone prefers Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo—arguably two of the world's greatest footballers today—may be associated with their political outlook, according to the results of an international survey led by Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore).

mix_items.title: World Vision România: Propunerea legislativă privind finanțarea ONG-urilor aduce măsuri disproportionate care riscă să afecteze copiii vulnerabili din România
mix_items.summary: Fundația World Vision România solicită Camerei Deputaților respingerea proiectul de lege privind finanțarea ONG-urilor, adoptat de Senat (L233/2026). Inițiativa legislativă... Articolul World Vision România: Propunerea legislativă privind finanțarea ONG-urilor aduce măsuri disproportionate care riscă să afecteze copiii vulnerabili din România apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Ionuț, elev în clasa a XI-a la un liceu din Municipiul Calafat, pasionat de muzică, cu un glas folcloric aparte,... Articolul Gândirea civică – concertul unei generații: povestea lui Ionuț, un tânăr în fața provocărilor cu care se confruntă tinerii de astăzi apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Bullet Cluster observations reopen dark matter debate with MOND-compatible explanation
mix_items.summary: The Bullet Cluster has so far been considered evidence of the existence of dark matter. An international team of researchers has now analyzed new data and current images from the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST). According to the team, the observations are also consistent with an alternative explanation that does not involve dark matter. If the latter is, in fact, present, it is likely to be in smaller quantities than postulated so far.

mix_items.title: Când dorința de a face bine este mai puternică decât emoțiile: Tiana și dragostea ei pentru voluntariat
mix_items.summary: Tiana are 21 de ani și face voluntariat de aproximativ 3 ani în programele și activitățile World Vision România. A crescut... Articolul Când dorința de a face bine este mai puternică decât emoțiile: Tiana și dragostea ei pentru voluntariat apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Robert are 18 ani și este reprezentant al copiilor în Consiliul Consultativ al Copiilor și Tinerilor din cadrul World Vision România. Prin... Articolul Schimbările mari încep cu pași mici: povestea lui Robert și drumul lui către advocacy la nivel internațional apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: For babies hospitalized with bronchiolitis, lying on stomach shows no clear benefit over lying on back
mix_items.summary: Placing a baby on their stomach in the prone position can help them breathe more comfortably. With the chest facing down and the back facing up, this position takes pressure off the lungs, allowing for better oxygen exchange and helping fluid drain more effectively. In a randomized clinical trial, named PROPOSITIS, researchers investigated whether the prone sleeping position could help very young babies with a lung infection called bronchiolitis, not to be confused with bronchitis.

mix_items.title: Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge
mix_items.summary: Alexandru are 10 ani, locuiește într-un sat din județul Giurgiu și este elev în clasa a III‑a. Este un copil cu... Articolul Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Engineered probiotic heads to human trials
mix_items.summary: A probiotic bioengineered by UBC Okanagan researchers to thrive in the inflamed gut is set to enter human clinical trials in Australia this summer, capping a decade-long effort to turn a beneficial bacterium into a treatment for inflammatory bowel disease.

mix_items.title: How animals communicate to work together across species boundaries
mix_items.summary: An international team of researchers have published a new review in Animal Behavior revealing how communication enables cooperation between different animal species. The review, titled "The ecology and evolution of cues and signals in animal interspecies cooperation," highlights how movements, visual displays, calls, and other behavioral cues and signals help partners coordinate interactions and align interests across species boundaries.

mix_items.title: Think you're eating healthy? You may be missing this heart-protecting nutrient
mix_items.summary: Eating five servings of fruits and vegetables may not be enough if you're missing foods rich in flavanols, a group of compounds linked to better heart health. Researchers found that choices like blackberries, plums, apples, broad beans, cherries, and green tea can dramatically increase flavanol intake.

mix_items.title: Examining the cardioprotective effects of heart failure treatments in patients with cancer
mix_items.summary: Patients receiving cancer treatment often face the added complication of side effects affecting the heart, which can lead to the need to discontinue anticancer therapy, reducing its effectiveness. Researchers from the Erasmus University Medical Center, Rotterdam, the Netherlands, presented their findings on the protective effects of medical therapies recommended to treat heart failure at ESC Cardio-Oncology 2026.

mix_items.title: Unearthed bathhouse reveals a thriving Roman Nijmegen: 'The Romans did not regard this city as a backwater'
mix_items.summary: Excavations in Nijmegen-West have uncovered large sections of a Roman bathhouse. It is the largest bathhouse complex from the Roman period in the Netherlands. Radboud researcher Stephan Mols can often be found at the excavation site. "The new finds show that the Romans did not regard this city as a backwater. The buildings were even larger and more imposing than we had previously thought."

mix_items.title: Scientists expected a black hole but found a neutrino factory powered by stars
mix_items.summary: A distant galaxy nicknamed Shadow Blaster may have revealed a surprising source of cosmic neutrinos: extreme star formation instead of a supermassive black hole. The discovery suggests that hidden, dust-filled starburst galaxies could account for a significant fraction of the Universe’s high-energy neutrinos.

mix_items.title: Student makes first recorded sighting of a microwhip scorpion in the Daintree Rainforest
mix_items.summary: A James Cook University Ph.D. student's late-night solo survey has led to the first recorded sighting of a microwhip scorpion in the Daintree Rainforest. JCU entomologist and taxonomist Matthew Connors works at the university's Daintree Rainforest Observatory as a demonstrator and rainforest tour guide for visiting groups.

mix_items.title: Small forest fragments can protect more birds when the surrounding landscape is more helpful
mix_items.summary: Larger areas contain more species. This is one of the most ironclad laws of ecology, which explains why large natural areas usually receive higher priority in conservation strategies. In fragmented landscapes, this logic has also led small forest fragments to be seen as environments of lower value for biodiversity.

mix_items.title: Researchers found a Wordle strategy that wins 99% of the time
mix_items.summary: Researchers developed a Wordle-solving strategy that succeeds 99% of the time by focusing on information gain rather than likely answers. The method uses Shannon entropy to identify guesses that reveal the most about the hidden word. Each guess is designed to slash uncertainty and narrow the possibilities faster. The result significantly outperformed more traditional Wordle tactics.

mix_items.title: Men make up less than 18% of Australian primary school teachers. Is this a problem?
mix_items.summary: This week, a group of Australian primary school principals called for more male primary school teachers, saying boys needed more "male role models."

mix_items.title: Why don't some people get vaccinated? It's more complicated than you think
mix_items.summary: When vaccination rates drop—as is the case with adult influenza vaccinations in Canada and child measles vaccinations in British Columbia—the explanation is often that people are "hesitant."

mix_items.title: Microbes destroyed an ancient pterosaur's wingbone, then preserved it for 100 million years
mix_items.summary: More than 100 million years ago, a flying reptile called a pterosaur flew over the oceans hunting squid and fish.

mix_items.title: Pilot study suggests cash support may help heart patients stay on medications
mix_items.summary: A pilot study led by UTSW researchers suggests modest financial support may help low-income heart failure (HF) patients stay on their medications after leaving the hospital. The study, published in the Journal of the American College of Cardiology, enrolled 153 patients at Parkland Memorial Hospital within two weeks of HF hospitalization and randomly assigned them to receive either usual care or a one-time $500 payment.

mix_items.title: New research finds major gaps between EU anti-deforestation rules and realities on the ground in Brazil
mix_items.summary: The U.K. could face significant challenges if it follows the European Union's lead and introduces tougher restrictions on imports linked to deforestation, according to new research from The University of Manchester. The study examines how the EU's landmark Regulation on Deforestation-Free Products (EUDR) is likely to work in practice in Brazil's beef sector, which is one of the world's largest sources of beef exports and a major driver of tropical deforestation.

mix_items.title: Scientists reprogram brain immune cells to fight Alzheimer’s
mix_items.summary: A newly identified molecule called OLE helped restore the brain’s immune cells to a more protective state in Alzheimer’s models. The treatment reduced toxic plaque buildup and improved memory, raising hopes for a new therapeutic approach.

mix_items.title: New tool maps how T cells move within tumors
mix_items.summary: Researchers at UT Southwestern Medical Center led a study to develop a computational method that reveals how immune cells navigate the complex environment inside tumors, offering insights that could inform future cancer therapies. T cells are key players in the body's defense against cancer, but many tumors create barriers that limit their ability to enter and function effectively.

mix_items.title: Einstein’s “biggest blunder” may finally have an explanation
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising connection between quantum gravity and an exotic quantum state of matter that could explain why the universe isn’t expanding wildly fast. The study suggests that the very shape of space-time may protect the cosmological constant from disruptive quantum effects.

mix_items.title: DNA time stamps reveal the strawberry’s surprising origins
mix_items.summary: Researchers have created a new way to reconstruct the evolutionary history of complex plant genomes by analyzing genetic traces left by transposable elements. The technique revealed that modern strawberries were assembled through multiple ancient genome-merging events, shedding new light on how major crop species evolved.

mix_items.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
mix_items.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

mix_items.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
mix_items.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

mix_items.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
mix_items.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

mix_items.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
mix_items.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

mix_items.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
mix_items.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

mix_items.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
mix_items.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

mix_items.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
mix_items.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

mix_items.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
mix_items.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

joke_ro.text: Mi-am luat sticla de apa ca sa fiu adult responsabil. Am umplut-o cu suc.

satire_ro.title: George Simion a spart geamurile la Digi24, să-l mai dea și pe el la televizor
